title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Initiative
subtitle: A path to shared research infrastructure at the University of Iowa
summary: A University of Iowa-facing presentation about turning Sutton SignWriting from fragile independent stewardship into durable shared research infrastructure.
synopsis: Assuming the immediate practical case for Steve Slevinski is already understood, this deck addresses the longer-horizon institutional question. Sutton SignWriting is already functioning as globally used infrastructure for signed language literacy, education, and research, but it remains structurally fragile. The deck emphasizes continuity risk, stewardship needs, research lanes, and the case for institutionally housed, grant-supported infrastructure at the University of Iowa.

links.label: Sutton SignWriting
links.description: Overview of Sutton SignWriting as a writing system for signed languages.

links.label: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy
links.description: Relevant policy context for building durable data and research infrastructure.

links.label: NIMH Data Archive
links.description: Example of the kind of external repository environment that motivates structured infrastructure and data stewardship.

slides.title: The Institutional Question
slides.summary: Assuming the practical case for Steve and a lab home is already understood, the institutional question is how SignWriting moves from fragile independent maintenance to durable shared research infrastructure.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for the infrastructure initiative overview.
bullets: Assume the practical case is already established
Built for long-term educational and research use
slides.speech: The Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure Initiative addresses the longer-horizon institutional question. Sutton SignWriting moves from fragile independent stewardship toward durable shared research infrastructure. It does more than preserve what exists. It creates a stronger institutional base for research, education, software stewardship, and future growth.
slides.notes: Open with the mission clearly and confidently, and make clear this deck is not re-making the practical case from zero.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Fragile Independent Maintenance] --> B[Institutional Home]
  B --> C[Grant Funding]
  C --> D[Durable Shared Research Infrastructure]

slides.title: What Sutton SignWriting Enables
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting is a writing system for signed languages that enables durable written, searchable, and research-usable signed language text.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for Sutton SignWriting overview.
bullets: Makes signed language writable and editable
Supports literacy, data, and structured reuse
Already used in education, language work, software, and research
slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is a writing system for signed languages. It makes signed language writable, editable, shareable, and structurally analyzable. That matters here because it creates forms of literacy and data that would otherwise remain harder to stabilize and study.
slides.notes: Orient quickly and emphasize the functional value that makes infrastructure possible.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Page of Sutton SignWriting symbols in a clean educational layout, paired with signing hands and written notation, academic style
slides.image_prompt_abstract: Signed movement crystallizing into written marks on a bright page

slides.title: Why It Matters As Infrastructure
slides.summary: This work matters because it touches literacy, accessibility, language science, cultural documentation, and the digital infrastructure needed to support signed language text.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for why Sutton SignWriting matters.
slides.speech: This work matters for several reasons at once. It matters for signed language literacy and access. It matters for language equity and disability inclusion. It matters for linguistics and multimodal representation. And it matters because durable infrastructure enables research that would otherwise remain fragmented or difficult to sustain.
slides.notes: Keep the value broad but crisp.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Sutton SignWriting] --> B[Literacy]
  A --> C[Accessibility]
  A --> D[Language Science]
  A --> E[Research Infrastructure]

slides.title: Current Global Use
slides.summary: This is not a hypothetical system; it is already used across countries, institutions, and communities, which means the infrastructure burden is already real.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for global use of Sutton SignWriting.
bullets: Existing tools, sites, and digital resources
slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is not only an idea waiting for first adoption. It is already in use across multiple countries and contexts. There are real users, real educational settings, and real digital resources that depend on ongoing stewardship and continuity.
slides.notes: Emphasize existing reality, not speculative possibility.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: World map highlighting multiple countries with SignWriting use, education, and digital tools, professional academic infographic
slides.image_prompt_abstract: Points of light across a world map connected by one language infrastructure network

slides.title: Why The Current Situation Is Fragile
slides.summary: The system already matters, but it remains vulnerable because the maintenance, documentation, tooling, and continuity model is still too narrow and person-dependent.
bullets: Continuity depends on too few people
Outages and disruptions have real consequences
slides.speech: The current situation is fragile because the system already carries real usage but still depends on too narrow a maintenance base. When continuity depends on too few people, disruptions become more likely, and each disruption affects real educational and research use.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Real Use] --> B[Too Few Maintainers]
  B --> C[Fragility]
  C --> D[Outage and Disruption Risk]

slides.title: Why The University Of Iowa
slides.summary: UIowa is a strong fit because the initiative crosses literacy, disability, linguistics, accessibility, data stewardship, and research support in ways the institution already understands.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for University of Iowa fit.
slides.speech: The University of Iowa is a strong fit because this work sits at the intersection of accessibility, literacy, Deaf education, linguistics, research systems, and international collaboration. It is exactly the kind of project that benefits from an academic home capable of connecting multiple kinds of expertise.
slides.notes: This is the fit slide, not the ask yet.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: University research ecosystem diagram connecting accessibility, literacy, linguistics, data workflows, and international collaboration
slides.image_prompt_abstract: Several academic domains converging into one central hub of shared infrastructure

slides.title: What Shared Research Infrastructure Means Here
slides.summary: Infrastructure here means stewardship, continuity, software maintenance, documentation, standards work, data readiness, and collaboration support rather than only code alone.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for what shared research infrastructure means.
slides.speech: Shared research infrastructure does not mean only a server or only a website. It means stewardship, continuity, software maintenance, documentation, data readiness, and the ability to support collaboration over time. The initiative is about building that fuller base so future research and educational use can rely on something durable.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Shared Research Infrastructure] --> B[Software]
  A --> C[Documentation]
  A --> D[Data Readiness]
  A --> E[Continuity]
  A --> F[Collaboration Support]

slides.title: Grant-Fundable Research Lanes
slides.summary: The initiative can support multiple research lanes including literacy, accessibility, standards, corpora, educational tools, and digital language infrastructure rather than only one narrow use case.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for grant-fundable research lanes.
bullets: AI, translation, and structured language data
slides.speech: One reason this initiative is promising is that it is not limited to one research lane. It can support literacy work, language representation, neuroscience and cognitive studies, and computational uses such as AI or translation. That diversity makes it more grantable and more collaborative.
slides.notes: This is the slide that helps people imagine future grants.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Four-lane academic research diagram connecting SignWriting to literacy, linguistics, neuroscience, and AI
slides.image_prompt_abstract: A central hub branching into four brightly defined research pathways

slides.title: The Near-Term Structure
slides.summary: The larger initiative becomes credible by growing from a lab home, a collaborator network, and grant writing under a PI rather than by trying to appear all at once.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for near-term structure of the initiative.
slides.speech: The initiative does not become credible by declaring itself all at once. It becomes credible by growing from a real structure: a lab home under a PI, collaborators across the University, grant writing under that PI, and complementary gifts or donations that support continuity and bridge needs.
slides.notes: Connect the mission deck back to the practical decks.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Lab Home Under PI] --> B[Collaborators]
  A --> C[Grant Writing]
  A --> D[Gifts and Donations]
  B --> E[Infrastructure Growth]
  C --> E
  D --> E

slides.title: Stewardship and Continuity
slides.summary: Long-term infrastructure requires stewardship, continuity, and a real human team behind the software, standards, documentation, and support systems.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for stewardship and continuity.
slides.speech: Durable infrastructure requires more than enthusiasm. It requires stewardship, documentation, continuity, and future-facing implementation work. The point is not only to keep systems alive in the short term, but to make them maintainable and usable across time.
slides.notes: Keep the audience thinking about institution-building, not heroics.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Academic infrastructure stewardship visual showing software, documentation, maintainers, and continuity planning as coordinated elements
slides.image_prompt_abstract: A durable scaffold supporting both current systems and future growth

slides.title: Funding Model
slides.summary: The strongest funding model combines grants as the primary lane with gifts and donations as legitimate but complementary support for continuity and special opportunities.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for infrastructure funding model.
bullets: Grants for research and infrastructure development
Salary support where project work justifies it
Gifts and donations for continuity and bridge support
slides.speech: A layered funding model is strongest. Grants are the primary lane for research and infrastructure development. Salary support makes sense where real project work is happening. Gifts and donations can provide continuity and bridge support. These lanes work best together rather than in competition.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Grants] --> D[Infrastructure Funding]
  B[Salary Support] --> D
  C[Gifts and Donations] --> D
  D --> E[Durable Initiative]

slides.title: Closing Opportunity
slides.summary: UIowa has the opportunity to help turn a globally used but still fragile system into durable research infrastructure with real scholarly and public value.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for the closing opportunity.
bullets: An academic home can change the trajectory
slides.speech: The opportunity is unusual and concrete at the same time. Sutton SignWriting already matters to real users and real educational and research contexts, but it remains structurally fragile. With an academic home, collaborators, grant writing, and careful stewardship, the University of Iowa can help change that trajectory.
slides.notes: End on opportunity rather than crisis.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Closing academic keynote slide showing global SignWriting use, university support, and a future-facing infrastructure pathway
slides.image_prompt_abstract: A fragile bridge becoming a durable illuminated structure
